Title: The mutation of ent-kaurenoic acid oxidase, a key enzyme involved in gibberellin biosynthesis, confers a dwarf phenotype to cucumber

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the oxidative conversion of ent-kaurenoic acid to GA12 during gibberellin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes stem elongation and influences shoot branching/plant architecture.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Changchunmici (CCMC); csdf mutant; mapping cross with Hazerd
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, male flowers, ovaries and tendrils; highest in ovary, abundant in tendrils and stems.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in various cucumber tissues, highest in ovary.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane (reported from transient GFP assay in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves); mutation altered localization.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 or GA4 treatment; flowering stage and seedling stage measurements.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss-of-function mutation in CsKAO decreases endogenous GA3, GA4 and GA12 levels and causes dwarfism with shortened internodes and reduced branch number/length; exogenous GA3 rescues the phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant identification; BSA-seq; map-based cloning; Sanger sequencing; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; LC-MS/MS hormone profiling; exogenous GA3/GA4 rescue; RNA-seq.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic effects include wrinkled cotyledons, smaller leaves, shorter ovaries and fruits, reduced branch number and branch length.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for breeding or engineering cucumber plant architecture via modulation of GA biosynthesis, with caution due to pleiotropic dwarfing and reduced branch/fruit traits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A G-to-A mutation in exon 6 introduces a premature stop codon, truncating the protein. The paper also notes another cucumber KAO-like gene (CsaV3_3G049400) showing differential expression, suggesting possible KAO family redundancy/feedback, but it was not the causal gene.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsKAO (CsaV3_6G006520) was identified by BSA-seq and map-based cloning as the causal gene of the csdf dwarf mutant. 2. A G-to-A mutation in exon 6 causes premature termination and likely disrupts ent-kaurenoic acid oxidase activity. 3. The csdf mutant showed significantly reduced GA3, GA4 and GA12 contents, linking CsKAO to GA biosynthesis. 4. Exogenous GA3/GA4 restored dwarf and branching defects, confirming that CsKAO deficiency causes GA-deficient architecture phenotypes. 5. Differential expression of other GA-pathway genes in csdf suggests feedback regulation after reduced GA levels.
